name: Medallions of Pork W/Red Wine Sauce
recipeIngredient: 8 Slices boneless loin of Pork, 3 oz each, trimmed of Fat
1/2 tsp Dried thyme OR 2 Springs of fresh Thyme
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound pork slices lightly with a mallet on a flat surface.
2. Sprinkle salt and pepper over the pork.
3. Combine ginger, thyme (or fresh thyme sprigs), vinegar, bay leaf, clove, red wine, and honey in a bowl and blend well.
4. Place sliced pork in a dish and pour the mixture over it. Marinate for 10 minutes.
5. Drain the pork medallions and pat dry.
6. Reserve the marinade.
7. Heat olive oil in a skillet and place meat in a single layer.
8. Cook for 5 minutes, then turn and cook for another 5 minutes on medium-high heat.
9. Reduce heat and cook meat for an additional 2 minutes.
10. Transfer meat to a dish and keep warm.
11. In the same skillet, add shallots and cook until wilted.
12. Add the reserved marinade and cook, stirring and scraping the bottom of the pan.
13. Cook until the marinade is reduced by about 3/4 of its original amount.
14. Swirl in butter and pour the sauce over the pork medallions.
15. Remove bay leaf before serving.
